title: Understand: Second Amendment: Text and History
description: Check your understanding of the text and history of the Second Amendment with this set of multiple-choice questions.
topic_path: Understand: Second Amendment: Text and History

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the exact wording of the Second Amendment to the U.S. Constitution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: “This amendment protects a private right of individuals to have arms for their own defense.”
"A well-regulated militia, being necessary to the security of a free state, the right of the people to keep and bear arms, shall not be infringed."
"No soldier shall, in time of peace, be quartered in any house without the consent of the owner."
"The right of the people to be secure in their persons, houses, papers, and effects, against unreasonable searches and seizures, shall not be violated."

assessment_items.question_content:
**What historical context helped influence the inclusion of the Second Amendment in the Bill of Rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Concerns over government censorship of speech and press
The desire to protect the authority of the states to maintain their own militias
Debates about how to regulate commerce between the states
The goal of ensuring fair and speedy trials for criminal defendants

assessment_items.question_content:
**What does the term "well-regulated militia" refer to in the Second Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A professional standing army maintained by the national government
Local law enforcement agencies
Organized groups of ordinary people trained to defend their communities
Private security firms hired by individuals

assessment_items.question_content:
**How did the English Bill of Rights of 1689 relate to the rights protected by the Second Amendment?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It established the principle of due process in criminal proceedings.
It prohibited cruel and unusual punishment.
It recognized the right of people to bear arms for self-defense.
It established the right to trial by jury in civil cases.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which TWO phrases are included in the Second Amendment’s __prefatory__ clause?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: “A well regulated Militia”
“being necessary to the security of a free State”
“the right of the people to keep and bear Arms”
“shall not be infringed”

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which TWO phrases are included in the Second Amendment’s __operative__ clause?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: “A well regulated Militia”
“being necessary to the security of a free State”
“the right of the people to keep and bear Arms”
“shall not be infringed”

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the purpose of the Second Amendment’s prefatory clause?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To nationalize security measures and give the federal government power to call on citizen-soldiers when necessary
To differentiate the wages given to soldiers who serve the national government and citizens who serve in militias
To express the founding generation’s commitment to self-defense through citizen-soldiers organized into militias
To give governments the power to regulate the types of weapons citizens had in their homes

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes the purpose of the Second Amendment’s operative clause?** 


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To guarantee individual firearm ownership and shield that ownership from federal interference
To place limits on the extent to which state governments can regulate the weapons a person owns
To differentiate between national regulations on weapons and state regulations on weapons
To define permissible personal weapons and describe federal power to restrict specific arms

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes what the founders meant by the term “standing army”?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A group of ordinary citizens who can be called upon to act as a military force in the defense of a state or local area
A military strategy in which a part of the army would be tasked with standing on the border to protect from foreign invasion
A group of officers who would be responsible for the care and safety of the president during meetings with other world leaders
A professional military force employed by the government for the purpose of national defense, even during peacetime

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best describes one of the founders’ key purposes for including the Second Amendment in the Bill of Rights?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: To legitimize the national government’s standing army
To protect against a tyrannical national government
To give states the power to regulate gun purchases
To determine which level of government would fund the army

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which statement best expresses the Federalist view of standing armies?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Standing armies are anti-republican and give the federal government too much power.
Standing armies are necessary to secure the liberties of the people and provide for a national defense.
Standing armies should be authorized only at the state level, as a national standing army would abuse its powers.
Standing armies must be put in place to legitimize the national government and force states to obey the Constitution.

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to the founders, what was the main difference between a standing army and a militia?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A standing army is controlled by the national government, while a militia is controlled by state governments or formed locally.
A standing army receives its funds from the government, while a militia is completely voluntary and not approved by any government.
A standing army focuses only on national defense in wartime, while a militia is a peace-keeping force for state governments to call on.
A standing army is authorized to act by both the national and state governments, while a militia usually emerges as a violent response to the government.
